title: The Oracle’s Shadow
subtitle: Intrigue, factions, secrets and consequences
description: Advanced sample campaign for testing narrative memory, factions, revealable secrets, tension clocks and session prep with consequences.
summary: The Triple Eclipse is approaching. Luminara prepares a public rite to consult the Oracle of Habits, but someone has replaced a page of the ceremonial oath. Every faction tries to use the crisis to confirm its own version of the future.
recommendedFor: DMs who want to test a dense campaign without preparing from scratch: factions with incompatible interests, clues that point to secrets, pressure clocks and consequences ready to trigger from player decisions.
pitch: A short fantasy intrigue campaign that shows how DMCC connects factions, clues, secrets and consequences. It is designed so a DM can open the dashboard, review the canvas, run four sessions and understand how narrative memory is preserved.
learningGoals: Use entities as a living map of NPCs, locations, factions, clues and clocks.
Prepare sessions by connecting goals, scenes, available clues and secrets at risk.
Separate what the DM knows from what the group can see in the player portal.
Record consequences so the world reacts after every session.
includedMaterial: Four prepared sessions with goals, scenes and table checklists.
Connected factions, NPCs, clues, secrets, rumors, decisions and consequences.
A narrative canvas for understanding the central conflict at a glance.
Public facts and DM secrets ready to reveal during play.

quickStart.title: How to start The Oracle’s Shadow
steps: Read the quick guide and open the Oracle canvas to understand which faction is pressuring each character.
Run session 1 with the goal of introducing Luminara, Iria and the first ink clue.
At the end of each session, mark which clues appeared and create a consequence if the group exposed someone publicly.
Use the editable copy to adjust names, factions or threat level for your table.

ent_tpl_oracle_campaign_note.title: How to use this sample campaign
ent_tpl_oracle_campaign_note.summary: This example teaches how DMCC preserves narrative memory: use the clock to create pressure, clues to guide without overexplaining, and consequences so every session leaves a mark.
ent_tpl_oracle_campaign_note.content: This campaign is designed to teach the DMCC flow: prepare a session, reveal clues, record decisions and leave pending consequences for the next game.

ent_tpl_oracle_city.title: Luminara, City beneath Three Shadows
ent_tpl_oracle_city.subtitle: Sanctuary-city where the Eclipse Council will gather
ent_tpl_oracle_city.summary: A ceremonial city, bright outside and fractured inside: temples, guilds and noble houses compete to control the interpretation of the Oracle.

ent_tpl_oracle_object.title: The Oracle of Habits
ent_tpl_oracle_object.subtitle: Artifact capable of showing repeated futures
ent_tpl_oracle_object.summary: It does not predict the future: it reveals the pattern that people will repeat unless they change in time.

ent_tpl_oracle_eclipse.title: Triple Eclipse Clock
ent_tpl_oracle_eclipse.summary: Each tick brings the city closer to a moment when hidden decisions become public.

ent_tpl_oracle_faction_pantheon.title: Pantheon of Habits
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_pantheon.subtitle: Priests who protect sacred routines
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_pantheon.summary: They claim social order depends on repeating the old rites exactly as written.

ent_tpl_oracle_faction_ink.title: The Fractured Ink
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_ink.subtitle: Network of scribes, forgers and forbidden memory
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_ink.summary: They want to alter the official chronicle before the Oracle turns it into accepted fate.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_iria.title: Iria Val-Sen
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_iria.subtitle: Archivist of the lesser temple
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_iria.summary: She knows a page of the rite was replaced, but fears that reporting it will trigger a purge.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_malvus.title: Malvus Corda
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_malvus.subtitle: Council adviser
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_malvus.summary: He speaks like a mediator, but pushes each faction toward choices that favor the altered rite.

ent_tpl_oracle_quest_open.title: Discover who altered the rite
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_open.summary: The group must connect archive clues, faction testimony and astronomical signs before the last shadow.

metadata.objective: identify the author, motive and consequence

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_ink.title: The ink ages differently
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_ink.summary: One line of the rite looks old, but reacts to heat like fresh ink.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_page.title: The replaced page reverses the oath
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_page.summary: The rite will not protect the city: it will force every leader to repeat their worst public decision.

ent_tpl_oracle_consequence_public_panic.title: Panic in the Council
ent_tpl_oracle_consequence_public_panic.summary: If the truth is revealed without complete proof, each faction will accuse the others and the city will lock down.

ent_tpl_oracle_scene_archive.title: Archive under the First Shadow
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_archive.subtitle: Opening investigation scene
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_archive.summary: The characters find an oath with a line that casts no shadow and a sealed archive that is too recent.
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_archive.content: Use this scene to set the tone: public liturgy, impossible documents and archivists who measure every word.

ent_tpl_oracle_scene_council.title: Hearing of the Eclipse Council
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_council.subtitle: Social pressure and rival factions
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_council.summary: Each faction offers a self-serving version of the truth while the ritual clock advances.
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_council.content: Dialogue scene. Give each faction one simple demand: control the discourse, expose the fraud or delay the rite.

ent_tpl_oracle_scene_chamber.title: Chamber of the Sealed Oracle
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_chamber.subtitle: End of the sample arc
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_chamber.summary: The Oracle waits beneath the sanctuary. It does not answer the future: it repeats unresolved habits.
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_chamber.content: Use this scene to close with a decision: reveal the manipulation, rewrite the oath or destroy the mechanism.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_tovan.title: Tovan, junior archivist
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_tovan.subtitle: Wants to do the right thing without ruining his mentor
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_tovan.summary: A young scribe who saw Malvus enter the archive late. He fears Iria will be blamed.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_tovan.content: Tovan whispers and remembers material details: resin smell, chrono-salt dust and a bell that rang out of time.

ent_tpl_oracle_faction_bells.title: Consortium of Bells
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_bells.subtitle: Guild controlling public signals
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_bells.summary: They want to delay the rite because an old prediction threatens their monopoly over signals and calendars.
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_bells.content: They are not direct villains: they prefer buying silence, creating doubt and turning any revelation into bureaucracy.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_chronosalt.title: Chrono-salt dust on the lectern
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_chronosalt.subtitle: Physical clue
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_chronosalt.summary: The oath lectern has silver crystals that appear only when a document has been artificially aged.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_chronosalt.content: Can be found by inspection, magic, scribal craft or questioning Tovan. Points to a recent substitution.

metadata.content: The document was aged with chrono-salt to look ceremonially old.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_no_echo.title: The oath without an echo
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_no_echo.subtitle: Ritual clue
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_no_echo.summary: One line of the text does not repeat when recited in the oath hall.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_no_echo.content: The false line reverses the duty to remember: it turns the rite into a repetition of errors.

metadata.content: The altered line is not part of the original oath and opens the central secret.

ent_tpl_oracle_item_key.title: Key of the Seven Habits
ent_tpl_oracle_item_key.subtitle: Ceremonial object
ent_tpl_oracle_item_key.summary: A bronze key with seven notches that opens the lower chamber if turned by following a sequence of daily habits.
ent_tpl_oracle_item_key.content: The key is not forced: it activates through simple city gestures — greeting, yielding, folding a page, touching a bell.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_iria_ink.title: Iria once helped the Fractured Ink
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_iria_ink.subtitle: Social secret, not current betrayal
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_iria_ink.summary: Iria leaked documents years ago to protect junior archivists, but she is not involved in the current substitution.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_iria_ink.content: This secret complicates the investigation: finding it too early can make her look guilty.

metadata.truth: Iria worked with the Fractured Ink in the past, but now she is trying to keep the city from breaking.

ent_tpl_oracle_rumor_bells.title: The bells dream names
ent_tpl_oracle_rumor_bells.subtitle: Street rumor
ent_tpl_oracle_rumor_bells.summary: Some neighbors say the bells pronounce names before each eclipse.
ent_tpl_oracle_rumor_bells.content: The rumor is a popular way to talk about the Consortium: someone is using public signals to point at suspects.

ent_tpl_oracle_decision_public_truth.title: Reveal the fraud in public or contain it?
ent_tpl_oracle_decision_public_truth.subtitle: Central campaign decision
ent_tpl_oracle_decision_public_truth.summary: The group can expose the false page before the city or handle the truth privately to avoid riots.
ent_tpl_oracle_decision_public_truth.content: There is no perfect answer. Fast exposure cleans the lie but inflames factions; containment buys time but looks like a cover-up.

ent_tpl_oracle_consequence_martial_rite.title: The Pantheon declares ritual emergency
ent_tpl_oracle_consequence_martial_rite.subtitle: Consequence if the city panics
ent_tpl_oracle_consequence_martial_rite.summary: The Pantheon restricts public gatherings and turns the investigation into a closed ceremonial matter.
ent_tpl_oracle_consequence_martial_rite.content: Use this if the group reveals the fraud without preparing allies, or if Malvus turns suspicion into unrest.

ent_tpl_oracle_handout_edict.title: Edict of Bell Silence
ent_tpl_oracle_handout_edict.subtitle: Public player handout
ent_tpl_oracle_handout_edict.summary: A document announcing three hours with no lesser bells during the eclipse. It looks administrative, but hides a political maneuver.
ent_tpl_oracle_handout_edict.content: Suggested text: By order of the Consortium, every lesser bell shall remain silent until the oath is read without shadow.

ent_tpl_oracle_encounter_masks.title: Procession of repeated masks
ent_tpl_oracle_encounter_masks.subtitle: Pressure encounter during the investigation
ent_tpl_oracle_encounter_masks.summary: A procession crosses the square wearing masks identical to leaders who committed historical mistakes.
ent_tpl_oracle_encounter_masks.content: Not mandatory combat. Use it to show that the Oracle is leaking past habits into public life.

rel_tpl_oracle_city_object.description: The Oracle is hidden beneath the central sanctuary.

rel_tpl_oracle_eclipse_object.description: The final phase of the eclipse awakens the artifact.

rel_tpl_oracle_pantheon_city.description: The Pantheon preserves Luminara’s ceremonial order.

rel_tpl_oracle_ink_pantheon.description: The Fractured Ink sees the Pantheon as a prison of narrative.

rel_tpl_oracle_iria_clue.description: Iria can guide the group toward the ink discrepancy.

rel_tpl_oracle_malvus_secret.description: Malvus knows about the replaced page and protects it.

rel_tpl_oracle_clue_secret.description: The clue does not solve everything, but opens the right question.

rel_tpl_oracle_secret_consequence.description: A badly managed revelation triggers political panic.

rel_tpl_oracle_quest_secret.description: Solving the quest opens the central secret.

rel_tpl_oracle_malvus_quest.description: Malvus redirects questions and offers comfortable answers.

rel_tpl_oracle_tovan_iria.description: Tovan wants to protect Iria, but his silences can look like complicity.

rel_tpl_oracle_tovan_chronosalt.description: Tovan knows the chrono-salt dust was not there the night before.

rel_tpl_oracle_noecho_secret.description: The oath without echo can prove which line was replaced.

rel_tpl_oracle_key_object.description: The key opens the lower chamber without breaking the ritual seals.

rel_tpl_oracle_bells_rumor.description: The Consortium feeds the rumor to steer attention toward specific names.

rel_tpl_oracle_bells_council.description: The Consortium demands delaying any decision until it controls the public signals.

rel_tpl_oracle_iria_past_ink.description: Iria hides her past help to the Ink because it could destroy her credibility.

rel_tpl_oracle_decision_consequence.description: A public revelation without allies pushes the Pantheon to close the investigation.

rel_tpl_oracle_masks_council.description: The procession turns political debate into a spectacle of collective guilt.

rel_tpl_oracle_chamber_object.description: The chamber reveals the Oracle as a machine of habits, not a divine voice.

rel_tpl_oracle_archive_chronosalt.description: The first scene contains the cleanest physical clue of the substitution.

rel_tpl_oracle_handout_bells.description: The edict lets the group suspect the Consortium without naming it directly.

rel_tpl_oracle_bells_object.description: The Bell Consortium wants to control the Oracle and turn its visions into public propaganda.

rel_tpl_oracle_tovan_clue_ink.description: Tovan recognizes the fractured ink and can explain why it does not belong to the original archive.

fact_tpl_oracle_1.statement: The Triple Eclipse has already begun, and three phases remain before the public rite.

fact_tpl_oracle_2.statement: The Oracle does not show a fixed future; it shows patterns people insist on repeating.

fact_tpl_oracle_3.statement: Malvus needs the group to suspect the Fractured Ink before reviewing the rite.

fact_tpl_oracle_4.statement: Iria will not betray the temple unless the group promises to protect the junior archivists.

fact_tpl_oracle_5.statement: If the Council panics, the Pantheon can lawfully close the investigation for ritual reasons.

fact_tpl_oracle_6.statement: The false page was aged with chrono-salt so it would look older than it is.

fact_tpl_oracle_7.statement: Tovan saw Malvus near the archive after the bells should have gone silent.

fact_tpl_oracle_8.statement: The Bell Consortium is not innocent, but it is not the only force moving the crisis.

fact_tpl_oracle_9.statement: The Key of the Seven Habits opens the chamber only when the city’s daily gestures are repeated in order.

fact_tpl_oracle_10.statement: Iria’s old link to the Fractured Ink can be used to frame her.

fact_tpl_oracle_11.statement: The mask procession is a symptom of the Oracle leaking repeated habits into public life.

fact_tpl_oracle_12.statement: The group’s final choice should decide whether Luminara remembers honestly, repeats its mistakes or buries the truth again.

sess_tpl_oracle_opening.title: Session 1 — The archive beneath the First Shadow

prep.summary: Open with investigation: an impossible document, a nervous archivist and the first sign that the rite was altered.
prep.openingPrompt: The first shadow falls over the archive skylight. The oath parchment should darken with the room, but one line stays bright as if the eclipse refuses to touch it.
goals: Present Luminara and its ceremonial pressure.
Introduce Iria and the altered ink clue.
Give the group a clear first question: who touched the oath?
prep.notes: Do not push toward a single solution. The point of the example is to record decisions and consequences.

chk_oracle_1.label: Show public tension without explaining the conspiracy yet.

chk_oracle_2.label: Give the group an emotional reason to trust Iria.

chk_oracle_3.label: End with a clear question: who do they show the page to?

prep.summary: A social session where factions pressure the group and the ritual clock advances.
prep.openingPrompt: The council chamber is arranged like a theater: every seat has a witness, every witness has a faction and every faction already knows who it wants blamed.
goals: Let players choose who they trust.
Reveal that delaying the rite has political cost.

chk_oracle_4.label: Ask what proof they show and to whom.

sess_tpl_oracle_masks.title: Session 3 — The procession of repeated masks

prep.summary: Pressure encounter and investigation escalation: the city starts showing consequences before the secret is solved.
prep.openingPrompt: The square fills with silent masks. Each face is familiar, not because of who wears it, but because of the mistake it repeats.
goals: Show the Oracle affecting public life.
Give the group another way to suspect Malvus or the Bells.
Force a choice between public action and quiet investigation.

oracle_s3_handout.label: Hand out or read the Edict of Bell Silence.

oracle_s3_tovan.label: Decide what Tovan knows and what he is hiding out of fear.

oracle_s3_clock.label: Advance the Triple Eclipse Clock if there is public exposure.

sess_tpl_oracle_chamber.title: Session 4 — The chamber that repeats habits

prep.summary: Final sample session: open the lower chamber, confront what the Oracle really does and choose what truth reaches the city.
prep.openingPrompt: The key turns only when every notch repeats a habit of Luminara: greeting, yielding, folding, ringing. Below, the Oracle is already whispering choices the city has made before.
goals: Reveal the nature of the Oracle.
Let the party decide what to do with the altered rite.
Create one lasting consequence for Luminara.

oracle_s4_choice.label: Prepare three endings: reveal, contain or destroy.

oracle_s4_malvus.label: Decide what Malvus offers to save himself.

oracle_s4_epilogue.label: Close with a visible change in the city.

cvs_tpl_oracle_map.title: Oracle tension map
cvs_tpl_oracle_map.description: Initial canvas for understanding clues, secrets and factions.

note_tpl_oracle_table.title: Table advice
note_tpl_oracle_table.text: Use this campaign to test the cycle: revealed clue → secret at risk → pending consequence.
